- [[dual-marching-cuboids]] -- A hybrid meshing algorithm combining greedy meshing with dual contouring, producing watertight meshes with adaptive polygon density using one vertex per merged cuboid.
- [[functional-representation-solid-modeling]] -- A solid modeling paradigm where 3D shapes are represented as mathematical functions f(x,y,z) rather than boundary representations, enabling watertight, manifold, hierarchical, and feature-preserving mesh generation.
- [[greedy-meshing]] -- A mesh optimization technique that merges adjacent voxel faces into larger polygons by greedily expanding in each dimension, drastically reducing polygon counts for block-aligned geometry.
- [[ilmola-generator]] -- A C++11 library for procedurally generating meshes of geometric primitives (spheres, boxes, cones, cylinders, torus, teapot, etc.) with a nestable modifier system and SVG/OBJ export.
- [[libfive]] -- Infrastructure for solid modeling using functional representations (f-reps), including a C++ library with C API, Guile Scheme and Python bindings, and a Studio GUI application.
- [[marching-cubes]] -- A computer graphics algorithm for extracting a polygonal mesh from a three-dimensional scalar field (voxel grid) by processing each cube independently with 15 base case patterns.
- [[marching-tetrahedra]] -- An isosurface extraction algorithm that subdivides each cube into tetrahedra before triangulation, producing meshes that match voxel data better than marching cubes but with more vertices.
- [[matthew-keeter]] -- Creator of libfive, an infrastructure for solid modeling using functional representations (f-reps).
- [[mesh-generation]] -- The process of generating a polygonal mesh from implicit surfaces (isosurfaces), point clouds, volumetric data, or parametric models for rendering, simulation, or 3D printing.
- [[meshreconstruction]] -- A fast, lightweight, self-contained C++14 Marching Cubes library with no dependencies, featuring precomputed lookup tables and narrow-band surface tracking.
- [[nii2mesh]] -- A minimal C tool that converts NIfTI 3D voxel images to triangulated meshes using Marching Cubes, supporting multiple output formats and mesh simplification.
- [[procedural-weapon-generation]] -- The algorithmic generation of weapons — swords, blades, firearms — using procedural techniques, typically for games, 3D printing, or digital art.

### methodology (2 pages)
- [[specs-to-code-criticism]] -- Critique of the 'specs-to-code' / vibe coding approach where developers write specifications and let AI generate code without understanding the output — arguing that the code must remain the developer's battleground.
- [[vibe-coding]] -- A prompt-driven development style where the developer describes what they want in natural language and the AI agent implements it, often with minimal review of the generated code.

### microgpt (1 pages)
- [[2026-06-11-karpathy-no-priors-interview]] -- Andrej Karpathy discusses auto research, open-ground distributed research, open-source vs frontier model dynamics, AI job market impact, microGPT, the AI education paradigm shift, and the bits-vs-atoms thesis on No Priors podcast.

### minimal-implementation (1 pages)
- [[micro-gpt]] -- Andrej Karpathy's 200-line minimal implementation of GPT training in Python, distilling the algorithm to its bare essence by stripping away all efficiency-related complexity.

### mixed-initiative (1 pages)
- [[constrained-synthesis-wfc]] -- WFC's ability to incorporate user-specified constraints, enabling autocomplete-style mixed-initiative design where the algorithm fills in parts of a partially constructed output.

### mkdocs (4 pages)
- [[2026-06-11-mkdocs-network-graph-plugin]] -- A plugin for Material for MkDocs that generates interactive D3.js network graph visualizations of documentation structure and page relationships.
- [[develmusa]] -- Open-source developer and creator of the MkDocs Network Graph Plugin for Material for MkDocs.
- [[material-for-mkdocs]] -- A popular Material Design theme for MkDocs static site generator, providing a modern documentation experience with extensive plugin support.
- [[mkdocs-network-graph-plugin]] -- An open-source MkDocs plugin that generates interactive D3.js network graph visualizations of documentation page structure and relationships.

- [[2026-06-11-mkdocs-network-graph-plugin]] -- A plugin for Material for MkDocs that generates interactive D3.js network graph visualizations of documentation structure and page relationships.

- [[mkdocs-network-graph-plugin]] -- An open-source MkDocs plugin that generates interactive D3.js network graph visualizations of documentation page structure and relationships.

### mmo (1 pages)
- [[realm-of-the-mad-god]] -- A bullet-hell MMO by Wild Shadow Studios (later DECA Games) that commissioned Amit Patel's Mapgen2 for procedural island map generation using Voronoi polygons and Whittaker biome classification.

### mmorpg (1 pages)
- [[2011-03-01-procedural-quest-generator]] -- Doran & Parberry (2011) analyze ~3000 quests from four MMORPGs to derive a common BNF grammar for quest structure, then build a Prolog-based procedural quest generator.

### model-synthesis (1 pages)
- [[ghx-proc-gen]] -- A Rust library for 2D & 3D procedural generation with Model Synthesis/WFC using AC-4 constraint propagation, available for the Bevy engine.

### models (2 pages)
- [[ai-speciation]] -- The predicted diversification of AI models into specialized niches optimized for specific domains — analogous to diverse brain specializations in the animal kingdom — as an alternative to the current monoculture of general-purpose models.
- [[frontier-vs-open-source-dynamic]] -- The relationship between closed frontier AI labs and open-source models, where open-source trails by 6-18 months but serves a critical role as a common, democratized platform for the industry.

### monetization (1 pages)
- [[2023-09-15-unity-runtime-fee]] -- Analysis of Unity's announced per-install Runtime Fee showing how unbounded install-based fees could exceed gross revenue for premium games. Unity later backtracked and capped the fee.

### monomyth (1 pages)
- [[joseph-campbell]] -- American comparative mythologist and author of The Hero with a Thousand Faces, which introduced the concept of the monomyth (Hero's Journey) — a universal pattern of adventure and transformation that underlies world mythology and is mapped by Dan Harmon's Story Circle.

### motivation (1 pages)
- [[npc-motivations]] -- A 9-category taxonomy of NPC motivations (Knowledge, Comfort, Reputation, Serenity, Protection, Conquest, Wealth, Ability, Equipment) derived from structural analysis of ~3000 MMORPG quests by Doran & Parberry.

### mri (2 pages)
- [[chris-rorden]] -- Neuroimaging researcher and open-source developer, creator of MRIcroGL, dcm2niix, nii2mesh, and niimath.
- [[nii2mesh]] -- A minimal C tool that converts NIfTI 3D voxel images to triangulated meshes using Marching Cubes, supporting multiple output formats and mesh simplification.

### msdf (3 pages)
- [[2026-06-11-sdf-msdf-fonts]] -- Amit Patel's comprehensive guide to Signed Distance Field (SDF) and Multi-channel Signed Distance Field (MSDF) font rendering for games and maps, with shader implementation details.
- [[multi-channel-distance-field]] -- An improvement over single-channel SDF font rendering that uses three distance fields (RGB channels) to preserve sharp corners, addressing the rounded-corner limitation of single-channel SDF.
- [[viktor-chlumsky]] -- Author of the msdfgen library and the 2016 paper on multi-channel distance fields (MSDF), providing the first complete open-source implementation of sharp-corner distance field font rendering.

### multi-agent-planning (2 pages)
- [[2018-08-19-conan-quest-generator]] -- Breault, Ouellet & Davies (2018) propose CONAN — a planning-based procedural quest generator that takes world descriptions as sets of facts and generates quests according to world state and character preferences, evaluated against the Doran & Parberry structural quest taxonomy.
- [[conan-quest-generator]] -- A planning-based procedural quest generator that takes world descriptions as sets of facts and generates quests according to world state and character preferences.

### mystery (1 pages)
- [[2021-02-08-mystery-and-wonder]] -- Game design analysis comparing Zelda: A Link to the Past vs Breath of the Wild, extracting four strategies for designing mystery: many-to-many relationships, ambiguous space classification, overlapping gameplay modes, and inside/outside integration.

### mythology (2 pages)
- [[joseph-campbell]] -- American comparative mythologist and author of The Hero with a Thousand Faces, which introduced the concept of the monomyth (Hero's Journey) — a universal pattern of adventure and transformation that underlies world mythology and is mapped by Dan Harmon's Story Circle.
- [[monomyth]] -- Joseph Campbell's universal narrative pattern found across world mythologies — a hero ventures into the unknown, faces trials, and returns transformed — which Dan Harmon mapped onto his 8-step Story Circle.

### narrative (7 pages)
- [[2026-06-11-dan-harmon-story-structure-series]] -- Dan Harmon's six-part tutorial series on story structure, presenting the circular 8-step 'Story Circle' model grounded in biology, psychology, and sociology, with practical application to Channel 101's 5-minute pilot format.
- [[harmon-story-circle]] -- An 8-step circular narrative model by Dan Harmon describing the universal pattern of story as a descent into the unknown and return — a character in a zone of comfort wants something, enters an unfamiliar situation, adapts, gets what they wanted, pays a price, returns, and has changed.
- [[monomyth]] -- Joseph Campbell's universal narrative pattern found across world mythologies — a hero ventures into the unknown, faces trials, and returns transformed — which Dan Harmon mapped onto his 8-step Story Circle.
- [[quest-giver]] -- The NPC character that offers a quest to players, provides information, and rewards completion — corresponding to Propp's dispatcher character role in narrative theory.
- [[quest-motif]] -- A semantic integrity constraint type that groups related predicates under a motif name, ensuring coherent goal generation across different branches of a branching quest by selecting goals sharing the same theme.
- [[story-structure]] -- The underlying framework and pattern of events that gives a story its shape, including models like the Harmon Story Circle, the Hero's Journey, three-act structure, and other narrative geometries.
- [[tension-story-arc]] -- A representation of narrative story arcs as sequences of tension changes — rise (+), fall (-), or maintain (=) — used as a target template in genetic-algorithm-based quest generation to evaluate plot quality.

### narrative-design (14 pages)
- [[adam-saltsman]] -- Game designer at Finji, creator of Canabalt and Overland, known for the plot vs. story distinction and the concept of froth.
- [[alex-epstein]] -- Narrative designer at Compulsion Games known for 'dirty narrative' — using intentional gaps and ambiguities in procedural storytelling in We Happy Few.
- [[benjamin-hill]] -- Co-creator of Duskers and co-developer of the curated narrative approach for procedural games.
- [[cat-manning]] -- Writer and game designer known for analyzing tarot as an analogue procedural storytelling system.
- [[chris-avellone]] -- Award-winning game writer and narrative designer known for Planescape: Torment, Fallout: New Vegas, and contributions to procedural storytelling theory.
- [[daniel-cook-spry-fox]] -- Co-founder of Spry Fox known for emotion engineering in games and amplifying themes through procedural systems.
- [[emily-short]] -- Interactive fiction pioneer known for authoring tools for procedural storytelling, Versu, Character Engine, and Tarot of the Parrigues.
- [[ink-scripting-language]] -- A narrative scripting language by Inkle for writing interactive fiction, featuring the Weave Structure for non-linear story flow with authored control.
- [[ink-weave-structure]] -- Ink's core narrative flow construct — an always-dropping-downwards system of knots, stitches, and diverts that prevents the player from retreating to earlier story states.
- [[jon-ingold]] -- Co-founder of Inkle and creator of the Ink narrative scripting language, the Weave Structure, and the concept of narrative momentum.
- [[narrative-momentum]] -- The feeling that things are always happening and progressing in a story — defined as importance × rate of change — achieved through architectural principles like Only Forwards Design and knowledge modelling.
- [[richard-rouse-iii]] -- Game designer and writer at Paranoid Productions known for heavily authored dynamic storytelling in Church in the Darkness.
- [[tim-keenan]] -- Co-creator of Duskers, known for curated narrative techniques that blend static content with procedural gameplay.
- [[zach-adams]] -- Co-designer and story writer for Dwarf Fortress, brother of Tarn Adams, whose narratives drive engine feature development.

### narrative-generation (46 pages)
- [[2018-08-19-conan-quest-generator]] -- Breault, Ouellet & Davies (2018) propose CONAN — a planning-based procedural quest generator that takes world descriptions as sets of facts and generates quests according to world state and character preferences, evaluated against the Doran & Parberry structural quest taxonomy.
- [[2019-01-01-procedural-storytelling-in-game-design]] -- An edited collection of 28 chapters by game industry practitioners on adapting traditional storytelling tools for procedurally generated game narratives, with case studies from Spelunky, Dwarf Fortress, The Sims, Frostpunk, Duskers, Caves of Qud, We Happy Few, and others.
- [[2026-04-28-llm-prompt-pipeline-quest-gen]] -- Borawski et al. (2026) propose a dependency-aware multi-stage LLM prompt pipeline for RPG content generation, decomposing the process into world building, NPC/PC creation, quest planning, and quest expansion — each stage conditioning on structured JSON from prior stages.
- [[2026-06-11-jason-grinblat-procedural-history-caves-of-qud]] -- Jason Grinblat's Roguelike Celebration talk on Caves of Qud's procedural history system, structured around five generated sultan histories, covering history-driven world generation and design constraints.
- [[2026-06-11-procedurally-generating-history-caves-of-qud]] -- Jason Grinblat's GDC talk on Caves of Qud's procedural history generation system — covering historical entities, replacement grammars for text, causality, and narrative coherence.
- [[authoring-tools-procedural-storytelling]] -- A framework for building tools that enable writers to create procedural narrative content — covering the first five minutes principle, sculptural writing, variety traps, and the founder principle.
- [[boiling-the-frog]] -- A gradual normalization design pattern where incrementally escalating moral compromises appear as logical conclusions — used in Frostpunk to explore authoritarianism.
- [[bruno-dias]] -- Creator of Voyageur and the Improv grammar library, known for the procedural descriptions filter stack balancing the oatmeal and brutalist building problems.
- [[caves-of-qud]] -- An open-world science fantasy roguelike RPG by Freehold Games (Jason Grinblat and Brian Butler), known for deeply procedural world generation, WFC-based map generation, and historical rationalization for narrative generation.
- [[computational-flaneur]] -- A site-specific generative poetry walk where GPS-triggered neural net text combines with physical environment to create emergent synchrony between generated content and real-world location.
- [[conan-quest-generator]] -- A planning-based procedural quest generator that takes world descriptions as sets of facts and generates quests according to world state and character preferences.
- [[curated-narrative]] -- Static, hand-crafted narrative content fragmented and reordered to coexist with procedural gameplay — creating different meanings through order and player choices.
- [[daniel-kline]] -- Game designer and scholar of dramatic gameplay who developed the four-fundamental framework for role-playing in games.
- [[dependency-driven-prompt-pipeline]] -- A multi-stage LLM prompting approach where each stage produces structured intermediate representations that serve as conditioned input to subsequent stages, reducing narrative drift and hallucinations.
- [[dirty-narrative]] -- Intentionally fragmenting story information and requiring active player interpretation through gaps, ambiguities, inconsistencies, and absences.
- [[dramatic-play]] -- A four-fundamental framework for role-playing in games: take on a role, make it personal, perform it, and the game recognizes it.
- [[emergent-narrative]] -- A narrative paradigm where stories arise bottom-up from the interaction of simulation systems and player actions, rather than being authored top-down — exemplified by Dwarf Fortress, The Sims, and other simulation-driven games.
- [[emotional-procedural-generation]] -- An approach to procedural generation that prioritizes genuine emotional connection over structural innovation, using sparse character detail to provoke player projection.
- [[ethical-procedural-generation]] -- A framework for ethical responsibility in procedural generator design — generators encode their creators' values and amplify them at scale.
- [[fail-forward]] -- A design principle where player failure advances the story rather than stopping it — failures change world state, trigger new events, or escalate tension.
- [[froth]] -- A LARP term for post-game storytelling where players excitedly narrate what happened during play — the social phase of gaming that games should design for.
- [[generative-poetry]] -- The algorithmic generation of poetic text using grammars, Markov chains, neural networks, or template systems to produce verse from formal rules or statistical models.
- [[heavily-authored-dynamic-storytelling]] -- A middle path between pure procedural generation and fixed linear narrative — randomized starting states with hand-crafted content that changes enough for players to notice.
- [[historical-rationalization]] -- A history generation technique where effect precedes cause — events generate a gospel that fabricates a reason retroactively, encoding the system's own ignorance about its world history.
- [[improv-grammar-library]] -- A grammar-based procedural text generation library by Bruno Dias with world model awareness — unlike context-free grammars (Tracery), Improv can query the game's world state to inform generation.
- [[jason-grinblat]] -- Co-founder of Freehold Games and co-creator of Caves of Qud, known for procedural history generation using historical rationalization, WFC map generation, and chunky procgen architecture.
- [[jim-davies]] -- Co-author of the CONAN procedural quest generation paper, researcher in AI and narrative generation at Carleton University.
- [[multi-stage-rpg-generation]] -- An architectural pattern for procedural RPG content generation that decomposes the process into sequential stages — typically world building, character creation, quest planning, and quest expansion — each producing structured data consumed by later stages.
- [[narrative-momentum]] -- The feeling that things are always happening and progressing in a story — defined as importance × rate of change — achieved through architectural principles like Only Forwards Design and knowledge modelling.
- [[only-forwards-design]] -- An architectural principle that prevents backtracking and dangling narrative loose ends at the system level — built into the Ink narrative language.
- [[plot-generator]] -- An algorithmic system that generates narrative plots — sequences of causally or thematically connected events — as opposed to generating individual quests, dialog, or character personalities in isolation.
- [[plot-vs-story-distinction]] -- Adam Saltsman's argument that games are plot generators (producing sequences of actions/events) while players are story generators (constructing narrative, motivations, and causality from plots).
- [[procedural-descriptions-filter-stack]] -- A filter-based architecture for procedural text generation that negotiates between a grammar and a world model to balance the oatmeal problem and the brutalist building problem.
- [[procedural-dialog]] -- A rule-matching dialog engine where increasingly specific rules override general ones, powered by a unified context dictionary of all game state — used in Left 4 Dead.
- [[procedural-dungeons-and-dragons]] -- Tabletop RPG frameworks — particularly Dungeon World's Moves, Dangers, and failing forward — as procedural content generation systems driven by human pattern recognition.
- [[procedural-storytelling]] -- The practice of using algorithmic systems to generate narrative content — characters, plot, dialog, history, world events — in interactive media, distinguished from purely mechanical procedural generation by its focus on narrative coherence and emotional impact.
- [[procedural-twitterbots]] -- Twitterbots as durational generative storytelling platforms that leverage Twitter's constraints — short messages, timeline context, profile metadata — to produce evocative narrative fragments.
- [[sebastien-ouellet]] -- Co-author of the CONAN procedural quest generation paper, researcher in AI and narrative generation.
- [[story-daemon]] -- An invisible system that injects narrative content into a game based on current game state — analogous to Left 4 Dead's AI Director but for storytelling.
- [[structured-llm-quest-generation]] -- A methodology for procedurally generating RPG quests using LLMs with structured intermediate representations, where quest content is produced through schema-constrained multi-stage prompting.
- [[tarot-of-the-parrigues]] -- Emily Short's tarot-based procedural storytelling game that adapts tarot's associative reading structure for interactive fiction.
- [[tarot-procedural-storytelling]] -- Tarot as an analogue procedural storytelling system that avoids the oatmeal problem through overlapping taxonomies of meaning requiring active interpretive construction.
- [[text-adventure-quest-generation]] -- The procedural generation of quests specifically within text-adventure games (interactive fiction), where quests are sequences of textual actions that must be semantically coherent and executable within the game state.
- [[tracery]] -- A grammar-based generation library by Kate Compton that enables recursive expansion of symbols into text — widely used for Twitterbots and procedural text generation.
- [[vincent-breault]] -- Lead author of the CONAN procedural quest generation paper, researcher in AI and narrative generation at the Science of Imagination lab.
- [[voyageur]] -- A game of procedural exploration and description by Bruno Dias, featuring a filter stack architecture for generating planet descriptions that balance perceptual uniqueness with coherence.

- [[mark-riedl]] -- Professor at Georgia Tech, Director of the Entertainment Intelligence Lab; researcher in AI-driven narrative generation, interactive storytelling, and game AI.
- [[prithviraj-ammanabrolu]] -- AI researcher focused on narrative intelligence, interactive fiction, and game AI; first author on quest generation in text-adventure games.

- [[dragn-town-quests]] -- A hybrid quest generation system developed by BYU's DRAGN Lab combining a Neo4j knowledge graph with a fine-tuned GPT-2 language model, published at CHI 2023.
- [[knowledge-graph-quest-generation]] -- A procedural quest generation approach that uses a knowledge graph (typically Neo4j) to encode semantic relationships between quest elements for coherent quest structuring and personalization.

- [[neural-quest-model]] -- A quest generation approach using LSTM-based encoder-decoder neural networks to learn the conditional probability of each action given the history of previous actions, capturing longer-range dependencies than Markov models.

- [[2019-09-13-text-adventure-quest-generation]] -- Ammanabrolu et al. (2019) present two approaches to procedurally generating quests in text-adventure games — a Markov model and an LSTM-based neural generative model — trained on recipe data to produce cooking quest action sequences.
- [[2023-04-19-dragn-town-quest-generation]] -- Knapp, Ashby & Fulda (CHI 2023) present a hybrid approach to personalized quest generation combining a Neo4j knowledge graph with a GPT-2 language model fine-tuned on World of Warcraft quest data.
- [[dragn-lab]] -- The Data Retrieval and Analysis Group for NLP (DRAGN Lab) at Brigham Young University, directed by Dr. Nancy Fulda, researching NLP, knowledge graphs, and procedural content generation.
- [[gpt2-quest-generation]] -- The use of a fine-tuned GPT-2 language model to generate quest dialogue, descriptions, and narrative text, typically conditioned on structured knowledge from a knowledge graph.
- [[gregory-knapp]] -- Researcher at Brigham Young University's DRAGN Lab, first author of the CHI 2023 paper on personalized quest generation using knowledge graphs and GPT-2.
- [[knowledge-graph-quest-generation]] -- A procedural quest generation approach that uses a knowledge graph (typically Neo4j) to encode semantic relationships between quest elements for coherent quest structuring and personalization.
- [[markov-quest-model]] -- A quest generation approach using Markov chains to model transition probabilities between actions, learning local coherence from a corpus of action sequences.
- [[nancy-fulda]] -- Professor at Brigham Young University, supervisor of the DRAGN Lab, and co-author of the CHI 2023 paper on personalized quest generation.
- [[personalized-quest-generation]] -- A procedural quest generation approach that adapts quest objectives, dialogue, difficulty, and rewards to individual player characteristics and preferences, typically using player state encoded in a knowledge graph.
- [[prithviraj-ammanabrolu]] -- AI researcher focused on narrative intelligence, interactive fiction, and game AI; first author on quest generation in text-adventure games.
- [[text-adventure-quest-generation]] -- The procedural generation of quests specifically within text-adventure games (interactive fiction), where quests are sequences of textual actions that must be semantically coherent and executable within the game state.

### noise (15 pages)
- [[2015-07-07-noise-terrain-maps]] -- Amit Patel's tutorial on using Perlin/Simplex noise functions to generate 2D terrain maps for games, covering elevation, biomes, climate, islands, and infinite terrain.
- [[2026-03-30-fast-and-gorgeous-erosion-filter]] -- Rune Skovbo Johansen presents a non-simulated erosion filter technique that produces branching gullies and ridges via gradient-aligned stripe noise, with improvements over the original technique by Clay John and Fewes.
- [[biome-maps]] -- A technique for assigning biomes using two independent noise maps — one for elevation and one for moisture — combined through a 2D lookup table.
- [[blue-noise-placement]] -- A technique using high-frequency noise to place irregularly spaced objects like trees and rocks by detecting local maxima.
- [[elevation-redistribution]] -- Techniques for reshaping noise-based elevation values using mathematical functions to create specific terrain characteristics like flat valleys or terraced hills.
- [[erosion-filter-technique]] -- A non-simulated terrain erosion technique that produces branching gullies and ridges using gradient-aligned stripe noise, where every point can be evaluated in isolation.
- [[fractional-brownian-motion]] -- A technique that sums noise at multiple octaves with decreasing amplitudes to produce fractal-like detail in terrain generation.
- [[infinite-terrain]] -- A locally-computed terrain generation technique where any position in an unbounded world can be generated on demand by offsetting noise coordinates by the camera position.
- [[island-shaping]] -- A technique for constraining noise-generated terrain to island shapes by combining noise elevation with a distance-based shaping function.
- [[noise-based-terrain-generation]] -- A methodology for generating 2D terrain maps using Perlin/Simplex noise as the primary building block, transformed through octave stacking, redistribution, and biome lookup.
- [[phacelle-noise]] -- A directional noise function extracted by Rune Skovbo Johansen from the erosion filter technique, producing gradient-aligned stripes with consistent magnitude via partial normalization.
- [[procgen-dart]] -- A Dart package by Andrew Brampton providing a collection of procedural generation algorithms — Perlin noise, fractal noise, Poisson disk sampling, Delaunay triangulation, Voronoi diagrams, and 2D geometry operations.
- [[ridged-noise]] -- A noise variant that uses the absolute value of noise to create sharp ridge features, often used for mountain ranges.
- [[rune-skovbo-johansen]] -- Creator of the LayerProcGen framework and erosion filter technique; indie game developer behind Eye of the Temple, The Cluster, and The Big Forest.
- [[wraparound-noise]] -- Techniques for creating tileable noise maps using cylindrical (3D) or toroidal (4D) noise coordinate projections.

### npc (6 pages)
- [[2011-03-01-procedural-quest-generator]] -- Doran & Parberry (2011) analyze ~3000 quests from four MMORPGs to derive a common BNF grammar for quest structure, then build a Prolog-based procedural quest generator.
- [[npc-motivations]] -- A 9-category taxonomy of NPC motivations (Knowledge, Comfort, Reputation, Serenity, Protection, Conquest, Wealth, Ability, Equipment) derived from structural analysis of ~3000 MMORPG quests by Doran & Parberry.
- [[procedural-quest-generation]] -- The algorithmic generation of quests for role-playing games, using structural analysis, grammar-based expansion, planning, genetic algorithms, knowledge graphs, or language models.
- [[quest-generator-prolog]] -- A prototype procedural quest generator written in Prolog with a Java applet front-end, using BNF grammar expansion and automatic backtracking to produce complex RPG quests.
- [[quest-giver]] -- The NPC character that offers a quest to players, provides information, and rewards completion — corresponding to Propp's dispatcher character role in narrative theory.
- [[questify-js]] -- A JavaScript procedural quest generator implementing Doran & Parberry's structural analysis approach with motivations, strategies, and atomic actions.

### open-brain (3 pages)

- [[nate-openbrain]] -- Creator of Open Brain, a structured SQL database approach to AI memory systems that emphasizes query-time synthesis, multi-agent access, and auditable provenance.
- [[open-brain-pattern]] -- Nate's structured database approach to AI memory: store information faithfully in a SQL database, synthesize fresh answers at query time, enabling multi-agent access and precise structured queries.

- [[2026-06-11-karpathy-no-priors-interview]] -- Andrej Karpathy discusses auto research, open-ground distributed research, open-source vs frontier model dynamics, AI job market impact, microGPT, the AI education paradigm shift, and the bits-vs-atoms thesis on No Priors podcast.

- [[frontier-vs-open-source-dynamic]] -- The relationship between closed frontier AI labs and open-source models, where open-source trails by 6-18 months but serves a critical role as a common, democratized platform for the industry.

### openai (1 pages)

### optimization (3 pages)
- [[connected-component-pathfinding]] -- A pathfinding optimization technique that tracks connected walkable regions via flood-fill indices, enabling agents to skip failed A* queries by checking component connectivity first.
- [[greedy-meshing]] -- A mesh optimization technique that merges adjacent voxel faces into larger polygons by greedily expanding in each dimension, drastically reducing polygon counts for block-aligned geometry.
- [[silhouette-gradient-descent-fitting]] -- A technique using gradient descent optimization with signed distance field (SDF) silhouette comparison to fit procedural model parameters to match reference 3D meshes.

### pathfinding (6 pages)

- [[connected-component-pathfinding]] -- A pathfinding optimization technique that tracks connected walkable regions via flood-fill indices, enabling agents to skip failed A* queries by checking component connectivity first.

- [[slope-penalty-pathfinding]] -- A pathfinding technique using non-linear slope penalties to generate natural-looking winding paths across terrain, avoiding steep shortcuts.

### procedural-generation (260 pages)
- [[10k-bowls-of-oatmeal-problem]] -- The phenomenon where mathematically unique procedural content feels perceptually identical — mathematical uniqueness does not equal perceptual uniqueness.
- [[2010-09-01-polygonal-map-generation]] -- Amit Patel's guide to generating game maps using Voronoi polygons instead of square or hexagonal grids, covering the full pipeline from polygon generation to rendering.
- [[2011-03-01-procedural-quest-generator]] -- Doran & Parberry (2011) analyze ~3000 quests from four MMORPGs to derive a common BNF grammar for quest structure, then build a Prolog-based procedural quest generator.
- [[2015-01-01-primer-random-numbers]] -- A comprehensive guide comparing random number generators vs random hash functions for procedural generation, explaining why hashes (especially xxHash) avoid correlated pattern artifacts that seeded RNGs produce.
- [[2015-07-07-noise-terrain-maps]] -- Amit Patel's tutorial on using Perlin/Simplex noise functions to generate 2D terrain maps for games, covering elevation, biomes, climate, islands, and infinite terrain.
- [[2015-10-10-procedural-world-approaches]] -- A taxonomy of three approaches to procedural world generation — simulation (process-based), functional (mathematical functions), and planning (top-down topology guarantees) — analyzing their tradeoffs for infinite chunk-based worlds.
- [[2015-11-21-study-in-composition]] -- A procedural generation jam entry creating landscape compositions that evoke different moods through L-system trees, HSV color palettes, and cinematographic camera cuts.
- [[2015-12-22-cluster-2015-retrospective]] -- Annual retrospective on The Cluster — an exploration-platformer with procedurally generated worlds, covering playtesting results, behaviour tree AI, pathfinding, dialogue systems, and world-structure communication.
- [[2016-03-01-natural-paths]] -- Using A*-like pathfinding on implicit coordinate grids with non-linear slope penalties to create natural-looking winding paths across procedural terrain, avoiding steep shortcuts.
- [[2016-04-05-puzzlegraph]] -- PuzzleGraph — a free open-source tool for analyzing and visualizing puzzle state spaces using graph theory to help designers understand solution paths, dead ends, and fail states.
- [[2016-05-03-the-big-forest]] -- First public reveal of the procedural infinite forest terrain project, using Perlin noise for tree placement with threshold-based clustering and footstep sounds.
- [[2016-09-06-cluster-on-hold]] -- Analysis of why The Cluster was put on hold after 10+ years: burdensome design constraints around no-getting-stuck guarantees, gravity without flight, AI-follow, and 3rd person art requirements.
- [[2018-08-19-conan-quest-generator]] -- Breault, Ouellet & Davies (2018) propose CONAN — a planning-based procedural quest generator that takes world descriptions as sets of facts and generates quests according to world state and character preferences, evaluated against the Doran & Parberry structural quest taxonomy.
- [[2019-01-01-procedural-storytelling-in-game-design]] -- An edited collection of 28 chapters by game industry practitioners on adapting traditional storytelling tools for procedurally generated game narratives, with case studies from Spelunky, Dwarf Fortress, The Sims, Frostpunk, Duskers, Caves of Qud, We Happy Few, and others.
- [[2019-06-03-dwarf-fortress-development-gamasutra]] -- In-depth 2019 interview with Tarn Adams covering Dwarf Fortress world generation, the staggered tick-based game loop, the body-part damage model (no hit points), tile rendering, and planned features including Steam release, magic systems, and economy.
- [[2019-09-13-text-adventure-quest-generation]] -- Ammanabrolu et al. (2019) present two approaches to procedurally generating quests in text-adventure games — a Markov model and an LSTM-based neural generative model — trained on recipe data to produce cooking quest action sequences.
- [[2019-11-01-lima-genetic-planning-quests]] -- Lima's 2019 PhD thesis at PUC-Rio presents the foundational method combining genetic algorithms with automated planning (STRIPS/HSP2) for procedural quest generation, including the Domain Database formalism, semantic integrity constraints, tree-structured genetic algorithm with state-compatibility crossover, and story arc fitness function.
- [[2019-12-12-houdini-procedural-assets]] -- Gatis Kurzemnieks introduces a 4-part series on building automated Houdini pipelines for procedural game asset generation, covering motivation, tool selection, and the Houdini PDG + Substance + RizomUV toolchain.
- [[2020-01-01-yu-quest-generation]] -- A paper on procedural quest generation from Nathan Sturtevant's group at the University of Alberta, likely applying search-based planning to generate game quests.
- [[2021-12-31-dwarf-fortress-development]] -- Interview with Tarn Adams covering Dwarf Fortress's ~711K-line C/C++ codebase, tech stack (MSVC, OpenGL, SDL, FMOD), pathfinding via A* with connected components, and the infamous drunken cat bug.
- [[2023-04-19-dragn-town-quest-generation]] -- Knapp, Ashby & Fulda (CHI 2023) present a hybrid approach to personalized quest generation combining a Neo4j knowledge graph with a GPT-2 language model fine-tuned on World of Warcraft quest data.
- [[2023-11-16-graphical-asset-generation-survey]] -- Fukaya, Daylamani-Zad & Agius (2023) systematically review 200 papers on procedural generation of graphical game assets, proposing a conceptual framework for PCG asset generation.
- [[2024-06-17-infinigen-indoors]] -- Raistrick et al. (CVPR 2024) present Infinigen Indoors, a Blender-based procedural generator for photorealistic indoor scenes with constraint-based arrangement.
- [[2024-08-18-the-cluster-released]] -- The Cluster, a 2.5D procedurally generated exploration platformer first prototyped in 2003, released for free on Itch.io after 21 years of on-and-off development.
- [[2024-10-02-dependency-graphs]] -- Development of a system that procedurally generates game progression dependency graphs and spatial graphs simultaneously, creating fully playable game levels with gates, keys, creatures, and clues.
- [[2025-01-05-2024-retrospective]] -- Yearly reflection: LayerProcGen released as open source, The Cluster finally released after 21 years, progress on The Big Forest's procedural creatures, and music releases.
- [[2025-01-10-procedural-creatures]] -- Rune's progress on procedural mammal generation (503 low-level params→106 high-level params) and procedural animation for The Big Forest, including gradient descent silhouette fitting, inverse kinematics overhaul, and reference animation analysis.
- [[2025-05-15-infinigen-articulated]] -- Joshi et al. (2025) introduce Infinigen-Articulated — a Blender-based toolkit for procedurally generating articulated assets across 18 categories for robotics simulation.
- [[2026-03-30-fast-and-gorgeous-erosion-filter]] -- Rune Skovbo Johansen presents a non-simulated erosion filter technique that produces branching gullies and ridges via gradient-aligned stripe noise, with improvements over the original technique by Clay John and Fewes.
- [[2026-04-28-llm-prompt-pipeline-quest-gen]] -- Borawski et al. (2026) propose a dependency-aware multi-stage LLM prompt pipeline for RPG content generation, decomposing the process into world building, NPC/PC creation, quest planning, and quest expansion — each stage conditioning on structured JSON from prior stages.
- [[2026-04-29-procfunc]] -- Raistrick et al. (2026) introduce ProcFunc, a Python library for Blender-based procedural 3D generation with function-oriented abstractions usable by VLMs.
- [[2026-06-11-branching-quests-genetic-planning]] -- Lima, Feijó & Furtado (2022) present a novel method combining automated planning with a genetic algorithm guided by story arcs to generate coherent branching quests for dynamic game worlds, evaluated via a Turing Test showing players cannot distinguish AI-generated quests from human-designed ones.
- [[2026-06-11-bryson-procedural-quests]] -- Joanna Bryson's work on procedural quest generation, likely presenting a structured approach to generating game quest content.
- [[2026-06-11-caius-walker-pcg-swords]] -- Caius Walker's BSc Honours project in Computer Games Technology implementing a procedural sword generator in C#/Unity using CSV part data with physics-based damage calculations.
- [[2026-06-11-delaunay-voronoi-sphere]] -- Amit Patel's technical guide to computing Delaunay triangulation and Voronoi regions on a sphere using stereographic projection, Fibonacci sphere points, and centroid alternatives.
- [[2026-06-11-end-to-end-procgen-caves-of-qud]] -- Jason Grinblat and Brian Butler present an architectural case study for composing multiple procedural generation techniques into large-scale generative systems, using Caves of Qud's village generation as an example.
- [[2026-06-11-game-dev-techniques-marching-cubes]] -- A Unity-based collection of game development algorithms including CPU and GPU Marching Cubes implementations with terraforming and noise-based terrain.
- [[2026-06-11-hexagon-tiling-sphere]] -- Amit Patel's exploration of the challenges and possible solutions for wrapping hexagon tile maps on a sphere, including the twelve-pentagon problem and coordinate system workarounds.
- [[2026-06-11-jason-grinblat-procedural-history-caves-of-qud]] -- Jason Grinblat's Roguelike Celebration talk on Caves of Qud's procedural history system, structured around five generated sultan histories, covering history-driven world generation and design constraints.
- [[2026-06-11-mapgen2]] -- Amit Patel's Mapgen2 island map generator, designed for Realm of the Mad God, using Voronoi polygons with coast-distance elevation and simple biomes.
- [[2026-06-11-mapgen3]] -- Amit Patel's abandoned Mapgen3 experiment — an attempt to simultaneously switch languages, data structures, and algorithms that failed due to changing too many variables at once.
- [[2026-06-11-moravaengine]] -- A C++ 2D/3D graphics engine supporting OpenGL 4.5+, Vulkan 1.2, and DirectX 11, with marching cubes as one of many implemented CG techniques.
- [[2026-06-11-procedural-face-generator]] -- Amit Patel's experimental procedural face generator using a five-dimensional hypercube parameter space for cartoon face expressions, inspired by Scott McCloud's Making Comics.
- [[2026-06-11-procedurally-generating-history-caves-of-qud]] -- Jason Grinblat's GDC talk on Caves of Qud's procedural history generation system — covering historical entities, replacement grammars for text, causality, and narrative coherence.
- [[2026-06-11-procgen-dart-library]] -- A Dart package by Andrew Brampton providing a collection of procedural generation algorithms — Perlin noise, layered fractal noise, Poisson disk sampling, Delaunay triangulation, Voronoi diagrams, and 2D geometry operations.
- [[2026-06-11-square-tiling-sphere]] -- Amit Patel's exploration of using a cube's six square faces as the basis for square tile maps on a sphere, covering gnomonic projection, area distortion, and coordinate systems for moving between cube faces.
- [[2026-06-11-terrain3d]] -- A Godot 3.5 project implementing procedurally generated 3D terrain using GDNative and C++, with LOD terrain tiles stitched together at mesh boundaries.
- [[2026-06-11-wave-function-collapse]] -- 
- [[2026-06-11-wfc-implementations-survey]] -- 
- [[2026-06-11-wfc-map-gen-caves-of-qud]] -- Brian Butler's GDC talk on how Caves of Qud uses Wave Function Collapse in texture mode for tile-based map generation, starting from hand-authored samples.
- [[adam-saltsman]] -- Game designer at Finji, creator of Canabalt and Overland, known for the plot vs. story distinction and the concept of froth.
- [[alex-epstein]] -- Narrative designer at Compulsion Games known for 'dirty narrative' — using intentional gaps and ambiguities in procedural storytelling in We Happy Few.
- [[alexander-raistrick]] -- Lead author of Infinigen Indoors (CVPR 2024) and ProcFunc (2026), researcher in procedural scene generation at Princeton.
- [[amit-patel]] -- Creator of Red Blob Games, author of Polygonal Map Generation for Games and influential tutorials on procedural map generation, pathfinding, and game algorithms.
- [[antonio-l-furtado]] -- Researcher at Pontifical Catholic University of Rio de Janeiro (PUC-Rio), Department of Informatics, contributing to procedural quest generation and automated planning for interactive storytelling.
- [[artist-in-a-box]] -- A generator design approach that reverse-engineers the process a human artist would follow and encodes it algorithmically — pioneered in Spore's creature editor.
- [[authoring-tools-procedural-storytelling]] -- A framework for building tools that enable writers to create procedural narrative content — covering the first five minutes principle, sculptural writing, variety traps, and the founder principle.
- [[autolevel]] -- A free Unity editor-integrated WFC-based procedural level generator with weight, volume, and boundary constraints.
- [[benjamin-hill]] -- Co-creator of Duskers and co-developer of the curated narrative approach for procedural games.
- [[biome-maps]] -- A technique for assigning biomes using two independent noise maps — one for elevation and one for moisture — combined through a 2D lookup table.
- [[blender-geometry-nodes]] -- A node-based system in Blender for procedural and non-destructive geometry creation and modification, integrated since Blender 3.0.
- [[blue-noise-placement]] -- A technique using high-frequency noise to place irregularly spaced objects like trees and rocks by detecting local maxima.
- [[body-part-damage-model]] -- A damage resolution system that replaces numeric hit points with a detailed anatomical hierarchy of body parts, each with material properties, enabling emergent narrative through specific, lasting injuries.
- [[boiling-the-frog]] -- A gradual normalization design pattern where incrementally escalating moral compromises appear as logical conclusions — used in Frostpunk to explore authoritarianism.
- [[boris-the-brave]] -- Creator of the DeBroglie WFC library, the chiseling method for generating connected structures, and adaptor of modifying-in-blocks for lazy infinite generation.
- [[branching-quest-generation]] -- Procedural generation of quests with branching storylines where player choices lead to different narrative paths, combining automated planning with genetic algorithms guided by story arcs.
- [[brian-butler]] -- Co-founder of Freehold Games and co-creator of Caves of Qud, known for pioneering WFC texture mode for tile-based map generation in commercial game development.
- [[bruno-dias]] -- Creator of Voyageur and the Improv grammar library, known for the procedural descriptions filter stack balancing the oatmeal and brutalist building problems.
- [[bruno-feijo]] -- Researcher at Pontifical Catholic University of Rio de Janeiro (PUC-Rio), Department of Informatics, specializing in procedural quest generation and interactive storytelling.
- [[caius-walker]] -- BSc Computer Games Technology graduate whose Honours project implemented a procedural sword generator in Unity with physics-based damage calculations.
- [[cat-manning]] -- Writer and game designer known for analyzing tarot as an analogue procedural storytelling system.
- [[caves-of-qud]] -- An open-world science fantasy roguelike RPG by Freehold Games (Jason Grinblat and Brian Butler), known for deeply procedural world generation, WFC-based map generation, and historical rationalization for narrative generation.
- [[chaikin-smoothing]] -- A corner-cutting algorithm for smoothing polylines and polygons by iteratively replacing each vertex with two new points at fractional positions along adjacent edges.
- [[chiseling-method]] -- A method by Boris the Brave for generating connected structures with WFC by carving out (chiseling) the output from a solid block rather than building it up from tiles.
- [[chunk-based-generation]] -- A world generation approach where the world is divided into rectangular tiles (chunks) that are generated independently on the fly.
- [[clay-john]] -- Creator of the original Eroded Terrain Noise Shadertoy (2018), the first implementation of the non-simulated erosion filter technique.
- [[computational-flaneur]] -- A site-specific generative poetry walk where GPS-triggered neural net text combines with physical environment to create emergent synchrony between generated content and real-world location.
- [[conan-quest-generator]] -- A planning-based procedural quest generator that takes world descriptions as sets of facts and generates quests according to world state and character preferences.
- [[connected-component-pathfinding]] -- A pathfinding optimization technique that tracks connected walkable regions via flood-fill indices, enabling agents to skip failed A* queries by checking component connectivity first.
- [[constrained-synthesis-wfc]] -- WFC's ability to incorporate user-specified constraints, enabling autocomplete-style mixed-initiative design where the algorithm fills in parts of a partially constructed output.
- [[constraint-propagation-ac4]] -- The constraint propagation algorithm used in WFC's propagation phase, based on the AC-4 arc consistency algorithm by Mohr and Henderson (1986).
- [[contextual-generation]] -- Procedural generation where operations produce seamless results across chunk boundaries while remaining deterministic.
- [[contradiction-winnowing]] -- A tag-based system for generating believable procedural characters by progressively eliminating conflicting trait combinations — used in State of Decay 2.
- [[convchain]] -- A related algorithm by Maxim Gumin that satisfies the strong version of condition C2 (exact pattern distribution matching) but does not guarantee C1 (only using input patterns).
- [[cube-sphere-projection]] -- A method for mapping a cube's surface onto a sphere by normalizing position vectors, used as a geometric foundation for square tile maps on spherical worlds.
- [[curated-narrative]] -- Static, hand-crafted narrative content fragmented and reordered to coexist with procedural gameplay — creating different meanings through order and player choices.
- [[damon-daylamani-zad]] -- Co-author of the 2023 systematic review on procedural graphical game asset generation.
- [[daniel-cook-spry-fox]] -- Co-founder of Spry Fox known for emotion engineering in games and amplifying themes through procedural systems.
- [[daniel-kline]] -- Game designer and scholar of dramatic gameplay who developed the four-fundamental framework for role-playing in games.
- [[darius-kazemi]] -- Independent game developer and creator known for the 'Keep It Simple' philosophy in procedural generation, banlists for ethical filtering, and Twitterbot culture.
- [[debroglie]] -- A C# library implementing the Wave Function Collapse algorithm with backtracking support, non-local constraints, and 2D/3D/hex grid support.
- [[decrews-quest-generator]] -- A C#/Unity implementation of Doran & Parberry's quest generation algorithm using Scriptable Objects for NPCs, locations, enemies, and items.
- [[dependency-driven-prompt-pipeline]] -- A multi-stage LLM prompting approach where each stage produces structured intermediate representations that serve as conditioned input to subsequent stages, reducing narrative drift and hallucinations.
- [[deterministic-generation]] -- The property that generating the same chunk always produces the same result, regardless of the order in which chunks are generated.
- [[dirty-narrative]] -- Intentionally fragmenting story information and requiring active player interpretation through gaps, ambiguities, inconsistencies, and absences.
- [[domain-database-quest-gen]] -- A formal description of the game world used in genetic-algorithm-based quest generation, comprising objects, ground literals, semantic integrity constraints, planning operators, and tension mappings.
- [[dominik-borawski]] -- Researcher and co-author of the dependency-driven prompt pipeline for coherent RPG generation (2026).
- [[downhill-river-generation]] -- A river generation technique where rivers start at random mountain corners and follow the steepest downhill slope through the Voronoi corner graph until reaching the ocean.
- [[dragn-lab]] -- The Data Retrieval and Analysis Group for NLP (DRAGN Lab) at Brigham Young University, directed by Dr. Nancy Fulda, researching NLP, knowledge graphs, and procedural content generation.
- [[dragn-town-quests]] -- A hybrid quest generation system developed by BYU's DRAGN Lab combining a Neo4j knowledge graph with a fine-tuned GPT-2 language model, published at CHI 2023.
- [[dramatic-play]] -- A four-fundamental framework for role-playing in games: take on a role, make it personal, perform it, and the game recognizes it.
- [[dual-graph-representation]] -- A map representation that stores both the Voronoi diagram (polygon shapes) and its dual Delaunay triangulation (polygon adjacency) together, enabling efficient switching between shape rendering and adjacency/pathfinding.
- [[edirlei-soares-de-lima]] -- Researcher in procedural quest generation, automated planning, and interactive storytelling at Universidade Europeia and UNIDCOM/IADE, Lisbon. Author of the foundational PhD thesis on GA + automated planning for quest generation (PUC-Rio, 2019).
- [[effect-distance-and-padding]] -- The mechanism by which chunks receive context from neighboring areas through padded data requests to lower layers.
- [[elan-ruskin]] -- Game developer and AI engineer who designed the rule-matching dialog system used in Left 4 Dead.
- [[elevation-from-coast-distance]] -- A terrain elevation model where elevation is defined as the distance from the coastline, producing monotonic elevation that increases inland with no local minima.
- [[elevation-redistribution]] -- Techniques for reshaping noise-based elevation values using mathematical functions to create specific terrain characteristics like flat valleys or terraced hills.
- [[emergent-narrative]] -- A narrative paradigm where stories arise bottom-up from the interaction of simulation systems and player actions, rather than being authored top-down — exemplified by Dwarf Fortress, The Sims, and other simulation-driven games.
- [[emily-short]] -- Interactive fiction pioneer known for authoring tools for procedural storytelling, Versu, Character Engine, and Tarot of the Parrigues.
- [[emotional-procedural-generation]] -- An approach to procedural generation that prioritizes genuine emotional connection over structural innovation, using sparse character detail to provoke player projection.
- [[entropy-heuristic-wfc]] -- The observation heuristic used in WFC where the NxN region with the lowest Shannon entropy is selected for collapse, producing organic-looking results by mimicking human drawing behavior.
- [[erosion-filter-technique]] -- A non-simulated terrain erosion technique that produces branching gullies and ridges using gradient-aligned stripe noise, where every point can be evaluated in isolation.
- [[ethical-procedural-generation]] -- A framework for ethical responsibility in procedural generator design — generators encode their creators' values and amplify them at scale.
- [[fail-forward]] -- A design principle where player failure advances the story rather than stopping it — failures change world state, trigger new events, or escalate tension.
- [[fast-wfc]] -- A C++17 implementation of Wave Function Collapse focused on performance, achieving ~10x speedup over the original implementation.
- [[felix-westin]] -- Refined the erosion filter technique visually in 2023 with a polished Shadertoy presentation, building on Clay John's original work.
- [[fibonacci-sphere]] -- An algorithm for distributing points approximately evenly on a sphere's surface using the Fibonacci sequence, with a spiral pattern that avoids clustering at poles.
- [[fractional-brownian-motion]] -- A technique that sums noise at multiple octaves with decreasing amplitudes to produce fractal-like detail in terrain generation.
- [[froth]] -- A LARP term for post-game storytelling where players excitedly narrate what happened during play — the social phase of gaming that games should design for.
- [[functional-approach-procgen]] -- A procedural generation approach where content is generated as a spatially local function without knowledge of surroundings, common in sandbox games like Minecraft.
- [[game-progression-dependency-graph]] -- A graph structure representing non-linear game progression where nodes are locations or objectives and edges represent dependency relationships (e.g., key required for door).
- [[game-tree-narrative]] -- A tree structure representing a game's narrative where each node is a branching quest, and each quest's outcomes determine which quests can follow, enabling coherent sequences of branching narratives.
- [[gatis-kurzemnieks]] -- Technical Artist and author of the 'Creating procedural game assets with Houdini' tutorial series, documenting a PDG-based pipeline for procedural game asset generation.
- [[generative-methods-taxonomy]] -- Kate Compton's classification of six fundamental approaches to building procedural generators: distribution, parametric, tile-based, grammars, constraint solvers, and agents/simulations.
- [[generative-poetry]] -- The algorithmic generation of poetic text using grammars, Markov chains, neural networks, or template systems to produce verse from formal rules or statistical models.
- [[geoffrey-card]] -- Game designer at Undead Labs known for procedural character generation systems in State of Decay 2.
- [[george-buckenham]] -- Creator of Twitterbots and durational generative art, known for analyzing Twitterbots as procedural storytelling platforms.
- [[ghx-proc-gen]] -- A Rust library for 2D & 3D procedural generation with Model Synthesis/WFC using AC-4 constraint propagation, available for the Bevy engine.
- [[gpt2-quest-generation]] -- The use of a fine-tuned GPT-2 language model to generate quest dialogue, descriptions, and narrative text, typically conditioned on structured knowledge from a knowledge graph.
- [[graph-rewrite-rule-procgen]] -- A technique using simple graph rewrite rules applied iteratively to generate directed acyclic dependency graphs with controlled branching factor.
- [[graph-wave-function-collapse]] -- An extension of Wave Function Collapse from grid structures to arbitrary graphs, using subgraph isomorphism (VF2) to extract and apply patterns.
- [[gregory-knapp]] -- Researcher at Brigham Young University's DRAGN Lab, first author of the CHI 2023 paper on personalized quest generation using knowledge graphs and GPT-2.
- [[harry-agius]] -- Co-author of the 2023 systematic review on procedural graphical game asset generation.
- [[hash-functions-procgen]] -- The use of fast hash functions (especially xxHash) in procedural generation for repeatable, uncorrelated pseudo-random values from coordinate inputs.
- [[heavily-authored-dynamic-storytelling]] -- A middle path between pure procedural generation and fixed linear narrative — randomized starting states with hand-crafted content that changes enough for players to notice.
- [[hello-sword-openscad]] -- A minimal OpenSCAD implementation of procedural sword blade generation by mugenZebra, producing parametrically-defined 3D blade geometry.
- [[historical-rationalization]] -- A history generation technique where effect precedes cause — events generate a gospel that fabricates a reason retroactively, encoding the system's own ignorance about its world history.
- [[houdini-procedural-asset-pipeline]] -- A Houdini-based automated pipeline for procedural game asset generation, using PDG to orchestrate geometry generation, texturing, and UV unwrapping across multiple tools.
- [[HSV-color-palette-generation]] -- Algorithmic generation of color palettes in HSV space using complementary, triad, or analogous hue relationships to establish mood.
- [[ian-parberry]] -- Professor of Computer Science at the University of North Texas, director of the Laboratory for Recreational Computing (LARC), researcher in procedural content generation and game development.
- [[ilmola-generator]] -- A C++11 library for procedurally generating meshes of geometric primitives (spheres, boxes, cones, cylinders, torus, teapot, etc.) with a nestable modifier system and SVG/OBJ export.
- [[improv-grammar-library]] -- A grammar-based procedural text generation library by Bruno Dias with world model awareness — unlike context-free grammars (Tracery), Improv can query the game's world state to inform generation.
- [[infinigen-procedural-scenes]] -- An open-source ecosystem of Blender-based procedural generators for photorealistic scenes, including outdoor natural scenes, indoor environments, and articulated assets for robotics simulation.
- [[infinite-terrain]] -- A locally-computed terrain generation technique where any position in an unbounded world can be generated on demand by offsetting noise coordinates by the camera position.
- [[internal-layer-levels]] -- A concept where a single layer class implements multiple layers at once, each at a different level of detail or abstraction.
- [[island-shaping]] -- A technique for constraining noise-generated terrain to island shapes by combining noise elevation with a distance-based shaping function.
- [[jason-grinblat]] -- Co-founder of Freehold Games and co-creator of Caves of Qud, known for procedural history generation using historical rationalization, WFC map generation, and chunky procgen architecture.
- [[jim-davies]] -- Co-author of the CONAN procedural quest generation paper, researcher in AI and narrative generation at Carleton University.
- [[joanna-bryson]] -- Professor of AI Ethics and Technology Policy at the Hertie School, Berlin. Known for AI ethics and robot ethics, with earlier work on procedural quest generation and intelligent agent behavior.
- [[jon-ingold]] -- Co-founder of Inkle and creator of the Ink narrative scripting language, the Weave Structure, and the concept of narrative momentum.
- [[jonathan-doran]] -- Researcher in procedural quest generation at the University of North Texas, co-author of the structural analysis of MMORPG quests and prototype quest generator.
- [[jongwoo-kim]] -- Game designer at Kitfox Games who developed the minimal trait-based personality generation system for The Shrouded Isle.
- [[jorgen-tjerno]] -- Game developer at Undead Labs who developed the contradiction winnowing system for procedural characters in State of Decay 2.
- [[jurie-horneman]] -- Game AI designer and ProcJam co-founder who developed the 'story daemon' concept for adaptive procedural storytelling.
- [[kaisei-fukaya]] -- First author of the 2023 systematic review on procedural graphical game asset generation.
- [[karhan-kayan]] -- Co-author of Infinigen-Articulated (2025), researcher in procedural generation of articulated assets for robotics simulation.
- [[kate-compton]] -- AI researcher and creator of Tracery, the grammar-based generation library; author of the '10,000 Bowls of Oatmeal' problem and the generative methods taxonomy.
- [[knowledge-graph-quest-generation]] -- A procedural quest generation approach that uses a knowledge graph (typically Neo4j) to encode semantic relationships between quest elements for coherent quest structuring and personalization.
- [[l-system-tree-generation]] -- The use of L-systems (Lindenmayer systems) for procedurally generating tree and plant geometry through iterative string rewriting.
- [[layer-based-procedural-generation]] -- A methodology for procedural content generation where generation processes are divided into multiple layers with strict separation between input and output of each layer.
- [[layer-dependencies]] -- The mechanism in layer-based generation by which chunks in one layer request and receive data from other layers.
- [[layerprocgen]] -- A C# framework for layer-based infinite procedural generation that is deterministic, contextual, and multi-threaded, designed for Unity but usable in any C# engine.
- [[lloyd-relaxation]] -- An iterative algorithm that redistributes points to produce more evenly spaced Voronoi polygon centers, reducing the clumpiness of random point distributions.
- [[malgorzata-giedrowicz]] -- Researcher and co-author of the dependency-driven prompt pipeline for coherent RPG generation (2026).
- [[markov-junior-web]] -- A browser-based TypeScript port of MarkovJunior with isometric rendering, .vox export, and experimental WebAssembly JIT compilation.
- [[markov-quest-model]] -- A quest generation approach using Markov chains to model transition probabilities between actions, learning local coherence from a corpus of action sequences.
- [[markovjunior]] -- A 3D simple tiled model implementation by Maxim Gumin that extends WFC to three dimensions with extensive tilesets and examples.
- [[marta-szulc]] -- Researcher and co-author of the dependency-driven prompt pipeline for coherent RPG generation (2026).
- [[matthew-bozarth]] -- Game developer at Undead Labs who contributed to procedural character systems in State of Decay 2.
- [[maxim-gumin]] -- Creator of the Wave Function Collapse reference implementation and related algorithms including ConvChain and MarkovJunior.
- [[michael-cook]] -- AI researcher at Queen Mary University of London known for his framework on ethical procedural generation and AI game design research.
- [[model-synthesis]] -- Paul Merrell's 2009 algorithm for generating larger models from examples by deriving adjacency constraints between tiles and using AC-3 constraint propagation.
- [[modifying-in-blocks]] -- Paul Merrell's technique for generating unbounded tile configurations by incrementally modifying the model in blocks, adapted by Boris the Brave and Marian Kleineberg for lazy infinite WFC generation.
- [[multi-stage-rpg-generation]] -- An architectural pattern for procedural RPG content generation that decomposes the process into sequential stages — typically world building, character creation, quest planning, and quest expansion — each producing structured data consumed by later stages.
- [[mx-lazer-walker]] -- Researcher at MIT Media Lab known for the Computational Flâneur — combining procedural generation with real-world embodied storytelling.
- [[nancy-fulda]] -- Professor at Brigham Young University, supervisor of the DRAGN Lab, and co-author of the CHI 2023 paper on personalized quest generation.
- [[narrative-momentum]] -- The feeling that things are always happening and progressing in a story — defined as importance × rate of change — achieved through architectural principles like Only Forwards Design and knowledge modelling.
- [[nathan-sturtevant]] -- Professor of Computing Science at the University of Alberta, known for pathfinding algorithms (HPA*, JPS), GVG-AI, and procedural content generation for games.
- [[neural-quest-model]] -- A quest generation approach using LSTM-based encoder-decoder neural networks to learn the conditional probability of each action given the history of previous actions, capturing longer-range dependencies than Markov models.
- [[noise-based-terrain-generation]] -- A methodology for generating 2D terrain maps using Perlin/Simplex noise as the primary building block, transformed through octave stacking, redistribution, and biome lookup.
- [[noisy-edges]] -- A rendering technique that replaces straight polygon borders with noisy, organic-looking lines constrained to quadrilateral regions defined by the dual graph, hiding the underlying polygon structure.
- [[npc-motivations]] -- A 9-category taxonomy of NPC motivations (Knowledge, Comfort, Reputation, Serenity, Protection, Conquest, Wealth, Ability, Equipment) derived from structural analysis of ~3000 MMORPG quests by Doran & Parberry.
- [[observation-propagation-cycle]] -- The core loop of the Wave Function Collapse algorithm that alternates between collapsing uncertain regions into definite states and propagating the resulting constraint information through the output.
- [[off-line-quest-generator]] -- A preprocessing subsystem that runs the quest generation genetic algorithm offline, producing the full set of branching quests for a game before runtime, which are then executed by the Game Manager during gameplay.
- [[only-forwards-design]] -- An architectural principle that prevents backtracking and dangling narrative loose ends at the system level — built into the Ink narrative language.
- [[oskar-stalberg]] -- Creator of Townscaper and Bad North, known for pioneering WFC on irregular grids, combining WFC with marching cubes, and developing custom observation heuristics.
- [[overlapping-model-wfc]] -- A WFC mode where NxN pixel patterns are extracted from the input bitmap and used as the basis for superposition states, enabling synthesis directly from example images.
- [[owned-within-bounds]] -- A pattern for disambiguating which chunk owns data that overlaps multiple chunk boundaries in procedural generation.
- [[pamir-bal]] -- Creator of the commercial Procedural Sword Generator for Blender, a geometry-nodes-based addon for generating swords and weapon assets.
- [[personalized-quest-generation]] -- A procedural quest generation approach that adapts quest objectives, dialogue, difficulty, and rewards to individual player characteristics and preferences, typically using player state encoded in a knowledge graph.
- [[phacelle-noise]] -- A directional noise function extracted by Rune Skovbo Johansen from the erosion filter technique, producing gradient-aligned stripes with consistent magnitude via partial normalization.
- [[piotr-mironowicz]] -- Researcher and co-author of the dependency-driven prompt pipeline for coherent RPG generation (2026).
- [[planning-approach-procgen]] -- A procedural generation approach where content is generated top-down with intent — planning connections, purpose, and meaning behind placed items.
- [[plot-generator]] -- An algorithmic system that generates narrative plots — sequences of causally or thematically connected events — as opposed to generating individual quests, dialog, or character personalities in isolation.
- [[plot-vs-story-distinction]] -- Adam Saltsman's argument that games are plot generators (producing sequences of actions/events) while players are story generators (constructing narrative, motivations, and causality from plots).
- [[procedural-animation-locomotion-system]] -- A kinematic approach to procedural character locomotion using forward kinematics, inverse kinematics, and data-driven gait parameters — developed as a Master's thesis by Rune Skovbo Johansen (2009).
- [[procedural-creature-generation]] -- The algorithmic generation of quadruped mammal anatomies using parametrized bone structures and extruded rectangle geometry, developed by Rune Skovbo Johansen for The Big Forest.
- [[procedural-descriptions-filter-stack]] -- A filter-based architecture for procedural text generation that negotiates between a grammar and a world model to balance the oatmeal problem and the brutalist building problem.
- [[procedural-dialog]] -- A rule-matching dialog engine where increasingly specific rules override general ones, powered by a unified context dictionary of all game state — used in Left 4 Dead.
- [[procedural-dungeons-and-dragons]] -- Tabletop RPG frameworks — particularly Dungeon World's Moves, Dangers, and failing forward — as procedural content generation systems driven by human pattern recognition.
- [[procedural-face-generation]] -- The algorithmic generation of cartoon faces from a parameter space, typically using a hypercube of control parameters for mouth shape, eyebrow position, and asymmetry.
- [[procedural-quest-generation]] -- The algorithmic generation of quests for role-playing games, using structural analysis, grammar-based expansion, planning, genetic algorithms, knowledge graphs, or language models.
- [[procedural-quest-generator-rust]] -- A Rust library for generating dynamic quests using a template system with variable replacement and validation.
- [[procedural-storytelling]] -- The practice of using algorithmic systems to generate narrative content — characters, plot, dialog, history, world events — in interactive media, distinguished from purely mechanical procedural generation by its focus on narrative coherence and emotional impact.
- [[procedural-sword-gen-blender]] -- A commercial Blender addon by Pamir Bal for procedurally generating swords, blades, and weapons using geometry nodes.
- [[procedural-twitterbots]] -- Twitterbots as durational generative storytelling platforms that leverage Twitter's constraints — short messages, timeline context, profile metadata — to produce evocative narrative fragments.
- [[procedural-weapon-generation]] -- The algorithmic generation of weapons — swords, blades, firearms — using procedural techniques, typically for games, 3D printing, or digital art.
- [[procfunclib]] -- A Python library for Blender-based procedural 3D generation using function-oriented abstractions, designed for human developers and vision-language models.
- [[procgen-dart]] -- A Dart package by Andrew Brampton providing a collection of procedural generation algorithms — Perlin noise, fractal noise, Poisson disk sampling, Delaunay triangulation, Voronoi diagrams, and 2D geometry operations.
- [[procjam]] -- A community game jam focused on procedural generation, co-founded by Jurie Horneman, that helped popularize procedural generation in independent game development.
- [[quest-generator-prolog]] -- A prototype procedural quest generator written in Prolog with a Java applet front-end, using BNF grammar expansion and automatic backtracking to produce complex RPG quests.
- [[quest-genetic-algorithm]] -- A genetic algorithm specialized for procedural quest generation, using tree-structured individuals representing branching quests, state-compatibility crossover, and story-arc-based fitness evaluation.
- [[quest-giver]] -- The NPC character that offers a quest to players, provides information, and rewards completion — corresponding to Propp's dispatcher character role in narrative theory.
- [[quest-motif]] -- A semantic integrity constraint type that groups related predicates under a motif name, ensuring coherent goal generation across different branches of a branching quest by selecting goals sharing the same theme.
- [[quest-structural-analysis]] -- A methodology for analyzing RPG quests by decomposing them into a formal BNF grammar with NPC motivations, strategies, and atomic actions, derived from corpus analysis of ~3000 quests.
- [[questgen-tiendil]] -- A Python library for automatic non-linear quest generation using directed graph representation with predicates, events, and constraints.
- [[questify-js]] -- A JavaScript procedural quest generator implementing Doran & Parberry's structural analysis approach with motivations, strategies, and atomic actions.
- [[questweaver-cpp]] -- A C++ quest/story generation system using weighted graph search with template files and serialization, separating quest logic from data.
- [[random-hash-vs-rng]] -- The distinction between hash functions and random number generators for procedural generation: hash functions produce uncorrelated outputs from different seeds, while RNGs with seeded sequences show correlated patterns.
- [[realm-of-the-mad-god]] -- A bullet-hell MMO by Wild Shadow Studios (later DECA Games) that commissioned Amit Patel's Mapgen2 for procedural island map generation using Voronoi polygons and Whittaker biome classification.
- [[riad-djemili]] -- Creator of Curious Expedition at Maschinen-Mensch, known for three-layer procedural storytelling through simple rules.
- [[richard-rouse-iii]] -- Game designer and writer at Paranoid Productions known for heavily authored dynamic storytelling in Church in the Darkness.
- [[ridged-noise]] -- A noise variant that uses the absolute value of noise to create sharp ridge features, often used for mountain ranges.
- [[robert-chudy]] -- Researcher and co-author of the dependency-driven prompt pipeline for coherent RPG generation (2026).
- [[rolling-grid]] -- A moving-grid data structure used by LayerProcGen layers to store and manage chunks as the generation focus moves through the world.
- [[rune-skovbo-johansen]] -- Creator of the LayerProcGen framework and erosion filter technique; indie game developer behind Eye of the Temple, The Cluster, and The Big Forest.
- [[sebastien-ouellet]] -- Co-author of the CONAN procedural quest generation paper, researcher in AI and narrative generation.
- [[semantic-integrity-constraints]] -- A set of six constraint types (t, opp, u, r, qualif, motif) used in genetic-algorithm-based quest generation to ensure generated branches respect the logical structure of the game world and produce coherent quests.
- [[silhouette-gradient-descent-fitting]] -- A technique using gradient descent optimization with signed distance field (SDF) silhouette comparison to fit procedural model parameters to match reference 3D meshes.
- [[simple-tiled-model]] -- A WFC mode where tile adjacency constraints replace pixel pattern matching, enabling tile-based level generation with explicitly defined tile sets and symmetry classifications.
- [[simulation-functional-planning-taxonomy]] -- A three-part taxonomy of procedural world generation approaches: simulation (process-based), functional (mathematical), and planning (top-down design).
- [[slope-penalty-pathfinding]] -- A pathfinding technique using non-linear slope penalties to generate natural-looking winding paths across terrain, avoiding steep shortcuts.
- [[spherical-voronoi]] -- Voronoi diagrams computed on a spherical surface, typically used for procedural planet generation. Can be computed via stereographic projection onto a plane for use with standard 2D Delaunay libraries.
- [[story-arc-fitness]] -- A fitness function for genetic-algorithm-based quest generation that evaluates branching quest quality by comparing all possible plot variants' story arcs to a desired target arc using mean squared error after scaling.
- [[story-daemon]] -- An invisible system that injects narrative content into a game based on current game state — analogous to Left 4 Dead's AI Director but for storytelling.
- [[structured-llm-quest-generation]] -- A methodology for procedurally generating RPG quests using LLMs with structured intermediate representations, where quest content is produced through schema-constrained multi-stage prompting.
- [[sword-maker-godot]] -- A Godot-based procedural pixel art sword generator using GLSL shaders and GDScript, by BumbertFiddlesticks.
- [[tanya-x-short]] -- Game designer, co-founder of Kitfox Games, and co-editor of Procedural Storytelling in Game Design.
- [[tarn-adams]] -- Creator of Dwarf Fortress, solo developer since 2002, co-editor of Procedural Storytelling in Game Design, known for deep simulation and procedural generation at unprecedented scale.
